intro: Secamone letouzeana is a species of plant in the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family, which is a type of climber or twiner native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including wound healing, pain relief, and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Secamone letouzeana include alkaloids, such as secamonine, secamone, and secamone alkaloids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer activities.
